channel.title: Kicksecure broken (kernel panic) with nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1 (discrete mode)
channel.description:
There are PCs were they have the option to boot with dGPU (e.g Nvidia) directly from the BIOS (called Discrete mode) instead of iGPU (CPU) called Hybrid Mode from BIOS.
This is useful for better display graphic and graphics related work.
KS will boot into black screen if only nvidia installed, this is the log:
``` [sysmaint ~]% journalctl -b -1 Hint: You are currently not seeing messages from other users and the system. Users in groups 'adm', 'systemd-journal' can see all messages. Pass -q to turn off this notice. May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd-xdg-autostart-generator[2049]: /etc/xdg/autostart/lxqt-panel.desktop:9: Key Name was defined multiple times, igno> May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Queued start job for default target default.target. May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd-xdg-autostart-generator[2049]: /etc/xdg/autostart/lxqt-panel.desktop:9: Key Name was defined multiple times, igno> May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Queued start job for default target default.target. May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Created slice app.slice - User Application Slice. May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Created slice session.slice - User Core Session Slice. May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Reached target paths.target - Paths. May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Reached target timers.target - Timers. May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Starting dbus.socket - D-Bus User Message Bus Socket... May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed dirmngr.socket - GnuPG network certificate management daemon. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed gcr-ssh-agent.socket - GCR ssh-agent wrapper. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed gnome-keyring-daemon.socket - GNOME Keyring daemon. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed gpg-agent-browser.socket - GnuPG cryptographic agent and passphrase cache (access for web browsers). May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed gpg-agent-extra.socket - GnuPG cryptographic agent and passphrase cache (restricted). May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Stopping gpg-agent-ssh.socket - GnuPG cryptographic agent (ssh-agent emulation)... May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Stopping gpg-agent.socket - GnuPG cryptographic agent and passphrase cache... May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed keyboxd.socket - GnuPG public key management service. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed pipewire-pulse.socket - PipeWire PulseAudio. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed pipewire.socket - PipeWire Multimedia System Sockets. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed gpg-agent-ssh.socket - GnuPG cryptographic agent (ssh-agent emulation). May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed gpg-agent.socket - GnuPG cryptographic agent and passphrase cache. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Removed slice app.slice - User Application Slice. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: app.slice: Consumed 780ms CPU time, 103.7M memory peak. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Reached target shutdown.target - Shutdown. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Finished systemd-exit.service - Exit the Session. May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Reached target exit.target - Exit the Session. May 14 14:06:43 localhost (sd-pam)[2042]: pam_unix(systemd-user:session): session closed for user sysmaint May 14 14:06:43 localhost (sd-pam)[2042]: pam_succeed_if(systemd-user:session): requirement "uid eq 0" not met by user "sysmaint" May 14 14:06:43 localhost (sd-pam)[2042]: pam_succeed_if(systemd-user:session): requirement "uid ne 0" was met by user "sysmaint" lines 108-141/141 (END) ```
So there are flags need to be added to make it compatible with discrete mode:
`sudo nano /etc/modprobe.d/nvidia-drm.conf`
add:
`options nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1`
Then regenerate:
`sudo dracut --force --regenerate-all`
The next reboot will lead to kernel panic, only way to bootup again it remove the flags from grub:
Press e on grub window, search for the line starting with "linux", and add to the end of it:
`rd.driver.blacklist=nvidia,nvidia_drm,nvidia_modeset,nvidia_uvm modprobe.blacklist=nvidia,nvidia_drm,nvidia_modeset,nvidia_uvm module_blacklist=nvidia,nvidia_drm,nvidia_modeset,nvidia_uvm systemd.unit=multi-user.target`
Then F10.
After that it will boot up, and you need to remove the added flags and regenerate dracut.
Note: I have tested that with Debian 13 DVD image with LXQT (default going to be initramfs and xorg) + Nvidia setup as before = it will bootup normally without issues.

item.title: Kicksecure broken (kernel panic) with nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1 (discrete mode)
item.dc:creator: nurmagoz
item.description:
Good catch, yep it did

Read full topic
item.source: Kicksecure broken (kernel panic) with nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1 (discrete mode)

item.title: Kicksecure broken (kernel panic) with nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1 (discrete mode)
item.dc:creator: Patrick
item.description:
Related:

Kicksecure – 13 May 26

panic-on-oops

Configure kernel panic behavior on oopses and warnings using panic-on-oops.service.

Read full topic
item.source: Kicksecure broken (kernel panic) with nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1 (discrete mode)

item.title: Kicksecure broken (kernel panic) with nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1 (discrete mode)
item.dc:creator: arraybolt3
item.description:
Does disabling panic-on-oops fix the panic issue? There’s a button in the sysmaint panel for that.

Read full topic
item.source: Kicksecure broken (kernel panic) with nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1 (discrete mode)

item.title: Kicksecure broken (kernel panic) with nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1 (discrete mode)
item.dc:creator: nurmagoz
item.description:
There are PCs were they have the option to boot with dGPU (e.g Nvidia) directly from the BIOS (called Discrete mode) instead of iGPU (CPU) called Hybrid Mode from BIOS.

This is useful for better display graphic and graphics related work.

KS will boot into black screen if only nvidia installed, this is the log:

[sysmaint ~]% journalctl -b -1                                                                               
Hint: You are currently not seeing messages from other users and the system.
      Users in groups 'adm', 'systemd-journal' can see all messages.
      Pass -q to turn off this notice.
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd-xdg-autostart-generator[2049]: /etc/xdg/autostart/lxqt-panel.desktop:9: Key Name was defined multiple times, igno>
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Queued start job for default target default.target.
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd-xdg-autostart-generator[2049]: /etc/xdg/autostart/lxqt-panel.desktop:9: Key Name was defined multiple times, igno>
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Queued start job for default target default.target.
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Created slice app.slice - User Application Slice.
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Created slice session.slice - User Core Session Slice.
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Reached target paths.target - Paths.
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Reached target timers.target - Timers.
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Starting dbus.socket - D-Bus User Message Bus Socket...
May 14 14:06:25 localhost systemd[2038]: Listening on dirmngr.socket - GnuPG network certificate management daemon.

May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Stopped target timers.target - Timers.

May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed dirmngr.socket - GnuPG network certificate management daemon.
May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed gcr-ssh-agent.socket - GCR ssh-agent wrapper.

May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed pipewire-pulse.socket - PipeWire PulseAudio.
May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed pipewire.socket - PipeWire Multimedia System Sockets.
May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed gpg-agent-ssh.socket - GnuPG cryptographic agent (ssh-agent emulation).
May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Closed gpg-agent.socket - GnuPG cryptographic agent and passphrase cache.
May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Removed slice app.slice - User Application Slice.
May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: app.slice: Consumed 780ms CPU time, 103.7M memory peak.
May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Reached target shutdown.target - Shutdown.
May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Finished systemd-exit.service - Exit the Session.
May 14 14:06:43 localhost systemd[2038]: Reached target exit.target - Exit the Session.
May 14 14:06:43 localhost (sd-pam)[2042]: pam_unix(systemd-user:session): session closed for user sysmaint
May 14 14:06:43 localhost (sd-pam)[2042]: pam_succeed_if(systemd-user:session): requirement "uid eq 0" not met by user "sysmaint"
May 14 14:06:43 localhost (sd-pam)[2042]: pam_succeed_if(systemd-user:session): requirement "uid ne 0" was met by user "sysmaint"
lines 108-141/141 (END)

So there are flags need to be added to make it compatible with discrete mode:

sudo nano /etc/modprobe.d/nvidia-drm.conf

add:

options nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1

Then regenerate:

sudo dracut --force --regenerate-all

The next reboot will lead to kernel panic, only way to bootup again it remove the flags from grub:

Press e on grub window, search for the line starting with “linux”, and add to the end of it:

rd.driver.blacklist=nvidia,nvidia_drm,nvidia_modeset,nvidia_uvm modprobe.blacklist=nvidia,nvidia_drm,nvidia_modeset,nvidia_uvm module_blacklist=nvidia,nvidia_drm,nvidia_modeset,nvidia_uvm systemd.unit=multi-user.target

Then F10.

After that it will boot up, and you need to remove the added flags and regenerate dracut.

Note: I have tested that with Debian 13 DVD image with LXQT (default going to be initramfs and xorg) + Nvidia setup as before = it will bootup normally without issues.

Read full topic
item.source: Kicksecure broken (kernel panic) with nvidia-drm modeset=1 fbdev=1 (discrete mode)
